title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
abstract: RT-2 casts robotic control as a vision-language-action sequence modeling problem and shows that web-scale vision-language pretraining can improve instruction following and semantic generalization for real robot manipulation.
raw_relevance_notes: Landmark VLA paper for mapping image + language + state to action tokens and interview-level discussion on semantic transfer.

title: Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models
abstract: Open X-Embodiment introduces a large multi-robot dataset and RT-X models for cross-embodiment transfer, highlighting the role of shared representations and data standardization in generalist manipulation.
raw_relevance_notes: Core for cross-robot generalization, dataset governance, and system-level interview questions.

title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
abstract: Diffusion Policy models multimodal robot actions with conditional denoising, enabling robust behavior cloning and high success rates in manipulation tasks with action chunking and receding horizon control.
raw_relevance_notes: Directly relevant to action representation, latency, and failure analysis in robot policy interviews.

title: DreamerV3: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models
abstract: DreamerV3 improves latent world model training and policy optimization for broad-domain reinforcement learning, offering a strong blueprint for model-based control and representation learning.
raw_relevance_notes: Foundational world model design with strong interview value on latent dynamics and planning tradeoffs.

title: A World Action Model for Embodied Planning with Latent Control Tokens
abstract: This seed paper entry represents the class of world action model research that learns action-conditioned latent transitions and supports planning with executable control abstractions.
raw_relevance_notes: Useful for practicing design reasoning around latent action planning even when no official PDF is available yet.

title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
abstract: DPO introduces a simple objective for preference optimization without explicit RL rollouts, deriving a stable update from chosen-rejected preference pairs relative to a reference model.
raw_relevance_notes: Must-know alignment baseline for comparing PPO/GRPO style post-training pipelines.

title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
abstract: DeepSeekMath reports strong mathematical reasoning gains through data curation and RL-style optimization, popularizing GRPO-style training discussions in open post-training workflows.
raw_relevance_notes: High interview value for practical RL post-training choices and verifier-driven reasoning loops.

title: Process Supervision Improves Reasoning in Large Language Models
abstract: Process supervision demonstrates that scoring intermediate reasoning steps can improve reliability compared to outcome-only supervision, motivating PRM-style reward design.
raw_relevance_notes: Critical conceptual bridge between PRM, ORM, and verifier-based post-training discussions.

title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
abstract: PaLM-E fuses language, vision, and robot sensor data into a multimodal embodied language model, enabling transfer across robotic and language tasks.
raw_relevance_notes: Important for interview questions on multimodal fusion and embodied transfer.

title: OpenVLA: An Open Foundation Model for Vision-Language-Action Robotics
abstract: OpenVLA provides an open VLA baseline for instruction-conditioned manipulation with reproducible training recipes and benchmarking protocols.
raw_relevance_notes: Useful for paper-to-implementation mapping and system design interview practice.
